key_insight: Explicitly sampling non-ideal configurational entropy, together with vibrational entropy, changes predicted binary phase stability and can shift transition temperatures substantially.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more physically faithful atomistic route to construct composition-temperature phase diagrams for alloy design and phase-stability prediction.
method: Alchemical thermodynamic integration with composition-dependent transformations
Monte Carlo identity-exchange moves to sample configurational entropy
Molecular dynamics to capture vibrational contributions along the free-energy path
evidence_signal: For Au-Cu, explicit configurational sampling lowered the AuCu order-disorder transition from about 810 K to 710 K for the LDA-trained ACE potential, closer to the experimental 683 K.
watch_item: Predictions remain strongly sensitive to the chosen exchange-correlation functional, so improved entropy sampling does not by itself guarantee quantitative accuracy.
paper_title: Computing binary alloy phase diagrams with explicit configurational and vibrational entropy
